name: Professor Peter Leyland
title: Senior Teaching Fellow in Comparative Public Law | College of Law | Researcher in comparative constitutional and administrative law
expertise_topics: ‘The Civil Service UK Style: Facing up to change?’ in K-P Sommermann
A Krywo and C Fraenkel-Haberle (eds) The Civil Service in Europe: A Research Companion (London
‘The UK’s Rwanda Asylum Policy and the Courts: reflections on the Constitutional Consequences’ DPCE online
‘Navigating the constitutional path to English Devolution: How not to crack an old chestnut in a disunited Kingdom’ Istitutuzioni del Federalismo
The Constitution of the United Kingdom: A Contextual Analysis (4th edn
‘The Origins of Thailand’s Bureaucratic State and the Consolidation of Administrative Justice’ in A Harding and M Pongsapan (eds) Thai Legal History: From Traditional to Modern Law
biography: Professor Peter Leyland specialises in public law and comparative public law. He is a visiting professor at the University of Bologna, University of Ferrara and Bocconi University in Milan but in addition has lectured at many universities and public institutions abroad including: the University of Victoria, Canada, the University of Melbourne, the Catholic University, Milan, University of Brescia, the University of Siena, the National Institute of Development Administration, Bangkok, the Office of the Council of State, Bangkok, Thailand’s administrative courts. He is a regular contributor to international conferences overseas.
research_interests: ‘The Civil Service UK Style: Facing up to change?’ in K-P Sommermann; A Krywo and C Fraenkel-Haberle (eds) The Civil Service in Europe: A Research Companion (London; Routledge; 2025); ‘The UK’s Rwanda Asylum Policy and the Courts: reflections on the Constitutional Consequences’ DPCE online; 2024; 767-782; ‘Navigating the constitutional path to English Devolution: How not to crack an old chestnut in a disunited Kingdom’ Istitutuzioni del Federalismo; 2023 Anno XLIV; Iuglio; settembre; 567-599; The Constitution of the United Kingdom: A Contextual Analysis (4th edn; Oxford; Hart Publishing; 2021); ‘The Origins of Thailand’s Bureaucratic State and the Consolidation of Administrative Justice’ in A Harding and M Pongsapan (eds) Thai Legal History: From Traditional to Modern Law; (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press; 2021
